oneLiner: A vibrant, premium protein drink for social occasions — the fun, feel-good choice when you go out.
positioningStatement: PEP is the refreshing protein drink designed for social settings, offering a premium, alcohol-free ritual that stands out in bars, rooftops, festivals, and dinners, making you feel good and have fun — without sacrificing taste or experience.
audience: People seeking non-alcoholic yet premium alternatives at bars, festivals, and dinners
Trendsetters who value aesthetics, experience, and inclusion in group rituals
Those who want a drink that feels as special and fun as a cocktail (but without alcohol)

voice.tone: Vibrant, inclusive, playful and polished; inviting everyone to feel special and join the moment.

visual.mood: Fun, premium, energetic, and festively social — meant for vibrant nightlife and stylish group occasions.
visual.photography: Aspirational, lively social scenes: people gathered at bars, rooftops, and events; hands holding beautiful drinks mid-laughter; dusk-to-night lighting with warm glows and energetic color pops.
visual.typography: Mix of bold Didot for headers/logo, Brittany Signature for slogans and accents (script), and Montserrat for body and stats — bringing together elegance, flair, and clarity.
visual.designConcept: fun, premium packaging, vibrant, festival vibe, fruity

inLineBrands.name: Kin Euphorics
inLineBrands.why: Delivers a social ritual and mood-enhancing, alcohol-free experience; focuses on aesthetics and belonging, just like PEP.

inLineBrands.name: Lyre's
inLineBrands.why: Sophisticated, elegant labeling for spirit-like, alcohol-free drinks, serving group occasions and social moments.

inLineBrands.name: Seedlip
inLineBrands.why: High-end, non-alcoholic drink designed for premium social experience, replacing traditional cocktails without compromising on flavor or visuals.
designPatterns: focus on social replacement for alcohol

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: HOLY
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Positioned for vitamin/functional health, not social enjoyment; design is too plain and health-centric.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: LMNT
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Hydration and electrolyte emphasis is functional and sporty, lacking the emotional and ritual focus for social settings.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: NOCCO
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Sports/BCAA energy focus targets athletes, not social or group rituals; bold and sporty not aligned with premium nightlife.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: PROTOCHOL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Mixes protein with alcohol (hybrid angle), but the fusion and packaging read more as a novelty than premium social; and the inclusion of alcohol is not congruent with PEP’s non-alcoholic social ritual.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: VITAMIN WELL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Clean, vitamin-hydration focus is health-led, not emotionally exciting for social occasions.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: BLOOM
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Prebiotic, friendly brand for wellness/lifestyle markets, less aligned with vibrant nightlife and social ritual energy.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: EVER TREE
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Minimal, natural and ‘subtle’ is elegant but not vibrant or group-experience focused; lacks the fun/festival vibe.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: OLIPOP
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Prebiotic, gut health orientation (wellness/lifestyle), and soft branding make it more everyday and health-forward than energetic and social-forward.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: POPP
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Wellness and health angle dominates; while vibrant, it’s more lifestyle/health-focused than specifically social or ritual-oriented.

antiReferences.name: Lean Body / gym RTD
antiReferences.why: Exudes ‘fitness-bro’ and performance energy — not a fit for stylish, inclusive social ritual.

antiReferences.name: drinkpep.com
antiReferences.why: Name collision; US brand, energy/probiotic focus (mismatch in taste, occasion, and mood — avoid confusion).

antiReferences.name: Generic sports drinks (e.g., NOCCO, LMNT)
antiReferences.why: Emphasize athletic performance, rational benefits, and bold/sporty styling — opposite of PEP’s playful, premium social vibe.

brandFitRules: Brand should evoke fun, premium, and social experiences—never gym, routine, or clinical functional vibes.
Social scenes, energy, and aspirational group moment visuals are musts.
Color palette must be vibrant or festival-inspired, not pastel, muted, or clinical.
Typography should mix refined and playful (Didot, Brittany Signature, Montserrat) — no techy, blocky, or sterile sans-serifs.
Exclude any visual/copy patterns that center on fitness, strict health, or data-first communication.
No references to protein quantity as a primary message—focus instead on taste, ritual, and joy.
Never use motifs, terms, or styling akin to drinkpep.com or gym RTD products.
Use script accents only for warmth and playfulness, not for childish/casual effect.
